segments.text: Wait, why America is burying millions of their tires under these highways? It looks like they're just hiding trash,
but this might actually be the future of how we are building roads. See, here is the problem. We have billions of old tires and they don't rot. Burning them
is toxic. So, someone asked, "What if we just buried them?" But hold on, road needs to be solid, but tires are bouncy.
If you drive a heavy truck over a bouncy foundation, the road should crack. But it does not because they don't just dump them. They create a honeycomb structure
filled with gravel. It catches the tire stopping the bounce. And this is the best part. It turns the road into giant shock absorbers. The tire eat the
vibrations from the truck. So the ashfelt stays on the top perfect. And why it is good for country? Because it cost half as much. It lasts twice as
long and it keeps toxic tires out of landfills. Such a massive
